channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Trump and officials 'likely' targets of press dinner shooting suspect, authorities believe
item.description: Authorities believe the press dinner shooting suspect targeted Trump and officials. The suspect will be charged with assault of a federal officer and using a firearm during a crime. The suspect is expected to be charged with assault of a federal officer and using a firearm during a crime of violence, officials have said.

item.title: Japan's JERA posts 5.2% rise in annual profit
item.description: JERA posted a 5.2% rise in annual profit. The Japanese company reported the increase in its latest financial results. Japan's JERA posts 5.2% rise in annual profit.

item.title: China's industrial profit growth quickens even as Iran war heightens risks
item.description: China's industrial profit growth has quickened. The growth occurred despite heightened risks due to the Iran war. China's industrial profit growth quickens even as Iran war heightens risks.

item.title: Iran Foreign Minister heads to Russia as talks remain stalled
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister is heading to Russia. Iran gave the US a proposal for reopening the Strait Of Hormuz and ending the war. Iran gave U. S. a proposal for reopening the Strait Of Hormuz and ending the war, says report.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: ‘Our math is different’, Iran warns Gulf countries
item.description: Iran warned Gulf countries of fourfold retaliation. The warning came after a threat from Trump amid stalled US-Israel-Iran war ceasefire talks. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: ‘Our math is different’, Iran warns Gulf countries of ‘fourfold’ retaliation after Trump threat The Times of India.

item.title: On the U.S. and India’s independent foreign policy
item.description: The US is limiting India's ability to pursue an independent foreign policy. The U. S. is slicing away at India’s ability to pursue an independent foreign policy. On the U. and India’s independent foreign policy.

item.title: Oil prices rise amid stalled US-Iran peace talks
item.description: Brent crude oil prices rose over 2 percent after the US and Iran failed to hold a second round of peace talks in Pakistan, stalling diplomatic efforts. The stalled talks led to an increase in oil prices.

item.title: Lebanon says Israel strikes kill 14 in deadliest day since truce
item.description: Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed 14 people, marking the deadliest day since a truce began. At least 36 people have been killed since the truce started, according to Health Ministry figures.

item.title: Shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner prompts scrutiny of Trump security arrangements
item.description: A shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has raised questions about US President Donald Trump's security arrangements. The security perimeter at the dinner was smaller compared to Trump's rallies.

item.title: Trump Event Shooter Names Kash Patel As Only US Official Off Target List
item.description: The shooter at a Trump event identified Kash Patel as the only US official not on his target list. The shooter, Allen, was taken into custody after running through a security checkpoint.

item.title: 10 Minutes Before Firing At Trump Event, Shooter Sent A Manifesto To Family
item.description: Allen sent his manifesto to his family 10 minutes before firing at the Trump event. His brother raised the alarm after receiving the manifesto. It was Allen's brother who first raised the alarm.

item.title: Justice Department cites dinner shooting to press preservationists to drop Trump ballroom suit
item.description: The US Justice Department cited a dinner shooting to urge preservationists to drop a lawsuit against a Trump ballroom. The department used the incident to make its case. Justice Department cites dinner shooting to press preservationists to drop Trump ballroom suit.

item.title: ‘I wasn’t worried… we live in a crazy world’: Donald Trump on shooting scare at Washington dinner
item.description: Donald Trump was unharmed in a shooting scare at a Washington dinner, where a person was taken into custody. The incident occurred at a private event, with Trump later saying he was not worried.

item.title: Israeli ‘ceasefire’ violations on the rise in Gaza
item.description: Israel has violated the Gaza ceasefire over 2,400 times, according to Al Jazeera. The violations have continued despite the ceasefire agreement in place. Israel has violated the so-called Gaza “ceasefire” more than 2,400 times.

item.title: Iran's foreign minister travels to Pakistan and Moscow after U.S. envoys' trip canceled
item.description: Iran's foreign minister traveled to Pakistan and Moscow after the US canceled its envoys' trip. The move comes amid stalled talks between the US and Iran over their war. Live Updates: Iran's foreign minister travels to Pakistan and Moscow after U. S.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes in Lebanon, as Iran Talks Remain on Hold
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah exchanged strikes in Lebanon, with tensions remaining high. Direct talks between the US and Iran to end their war have stalled. Cease-fires in Lebanon and Iran are on shaky ground, with military attacks flaring and direct talks between Washington and Tehran to end their war stalled.

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals merge in bid to oust his government
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's rivals merged to oust his government. Two opposition groups joined forces to challenge his coalition. Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's biggest rivals have announced they are joining forces to attempt to oust his coalition government.

item.title: Iran’s foreign minister leaves Pakistan, heads to Russia for more talks
item.description: Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi travelled to Russia for talks with senior officials. Abbas Araghchi travels to Russia, where he will speak with "senior officials", Iran's foreign ministry says. Iran’s foreign minister leaves Pakistan, heads to Russia for more talks.

item.title: "I Want To Throw Up, Cry": What Trump Event Shooter Wrote In His Manifesto
item.description: Allen, 31, from Torrance, California, was taken into custody after a shooting at a Donald Trump event. He wrote in his manifesto that he wanted to throw up and cry.

item.title: Trump Event Shooter Mocked Hotel Security In Manifesto, Said 'Won't Use Slugs'
item.description: The shooter who targeted a Trump event mocked hotel security in his manifesto, mentioning he wouldn't use "slugs". Officials believed he acted alone, labeling him a "lone wolf". Trump said officials believed he acted alone and was what he called a "lone wolf".
